metadata_information.title: An integrated model of perennial and annual crop production for Sub-Saharan countries
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: An integrated model of perennial and annual crop production for Sub-Saharan countries

document_description.abstract: Crop production in sub-Saharan Africa is dominated by smallholders who allocate household labor across annual and perennial crops and, in some cases, to wage labor markets. This paper develops a microeconomic model of household choice which is consistent with observed characteristics of sub-Saharan agricultural systems in terms of: integrated production of annual and perennial crops; price uncertainty in markets that may be affected by government intervention; the potential for off-farm employment; household consumption of crops produced on the farm; and household consumption of non-food goods, school fees and so on. The paper considers variations in the model to establish their implications. These variations include differential buying and selling prices, fixed subsistence consumption constraints, participation in wage market labor, smuggling in response to government price control, and parallel markets with penalties for smuggling.

document_description.disclaimer: The Policy Research Working Paper Series disseminates the findings of work in progress to encourage the exchange of ideas about development issues. An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
